data.title: SLB in charts: Q3 North America revenue +14%, Latin America -12% Y/Y
Samsung to unveil its first trifold phone this month: report
data.description:
Samsung Electronics (OTCPK:SSNLF) is set to unveil its first trifold smartphone on the sidelines of the Asia-Pacific Economic Cooperation, or APEC, summit in South Korea this month, Bloomberg News reported.

It will be the company’s first device with two hinges, allowing it to work as either a conventional smartphone or a larger tablet when fully unfurled, and will be displayed at an exhibition of cutting-edge Korean technology, on the sidelines of the event, the report added, citing people with knowledge of the matter.

Samsung did not immediately respond to a request for comment from Seeking Alpha.

Alongside Huawei Technologies, Samsung has led the move to develop foldable phones. In September last year, Huawei launched its Z-shaped tri-fold phone Mate XT, and last month introduced an update to the phone. 

Attendees at this month’s event likely would not be allowed to touch the new device, as it is expected to be displayed under glass. The full commercial launch of the trifold phone is expected to occur later this year, the report noted.

Samsung’s seventh-generation book-style foldable Galaxy Z Fold 7 was launched in July and has seen success with consumers due to its thinner and lighter design, the report added.

Apple (AAPL) is expected to introduce its first folding iPhone next year.

data.title: Earnings Snapshot: Truist Financial tops Q3 estimates; reaffirms FY adj. revenue and adj. expenses outlook
Huntington Bancshares GAAP EPS of $0.41 beats by $0.03
data.description:
• Huntington Bancshares press release (NASDAQ:HBAN): Q3 GAAP EPS of $0.41 beats by $0.03.
  • Average total loans and leases increased $2.8 billion, or 2%, from the prior quarter to $135.9 billion, and increased $11.4 billion, or 9%, from the year-ago quarter.
    • Average commercial loans grew $2.0 billion, or 3%, from the prior quarter and $8.5 billion, or 12%, from the year-ago quarter.
    • Average consumer loans grew $794 million, or 1%, from the prior quarter and $2.9 billion, or 5%, from the year-ago quarter.
  • Average total deposits increased $1.4 billion, or 1%, from the prior quarter and $8.3 billion, or 5%, from the year-ago quarter.
  • Net charge-offs of 0.22% of average total loans and leases for the quarter, 2 basis points higher than the prior quarter.
  • Nonperforming asset ratio of 0.60% at quarter end, 3 basis points lower than the prior quarter.
  • Allowance for credit losses (ACL) of $2.6 billion, or 1.86% of total loans and leases, at quarter end, an increase of $47 million from the prior quarter.
  • Common Equity Tier 1 (CET1) risk-based capital ratio was 10.6%, at September 30, 2025, compared to 10.5% in the prior quarter.
  • Tangible common equity (TCE) ratio of 6.8%, up from 6.6% in the prior quarter and 6.4% from a year ago.
  • Tangible book value per share of $9.54, up $0.41, or 4%, from the prior quarter and up $0.89, or 10%, from a year ago.

data.title: Dutch Bros increases its focus on food in new growth strategy
data.description:
Dutch Bros (NYSE:BROS) is making a strong move into offering food items this year, including the introduction of a chorizo wrap as part of a test breakfast menu rollout. The chorizo wrap is one of four core items that were added to Dutch Bros' evolving breakfast offerings in limited shops.

The restaurant operator has not announced a system-wide, permanent launch for all stores, so availability of the chorizo wrap and other new food items may still vary by region.

TD Cowen analyst Andrew Charles said Dutch Bros (NYSE:BROS) expanded food lineup is a key driver behind the firm's same-store sales forecast on BROS that is above the consensus estimate. "We were pleased with the wrap's heartiness and flavor, and expect the expanded food line should drive strong attachment to beverages," wrote Charles about his test of the new menu item.

Notably, Dutch Bros (BROS) had a meager 2% food mix as a percentage of sales at the end of last year, which was well below the food mix of Starbucks (SBUX) at 23% and Black Rock Coffee Bar (BRCB) at 11%. Looking ahead, Dutch Bros (BROS) management has said that the expanded food rollout will be completed throughout 2026. In addition to the chorizo wrap, the chain is expected to introduce breakfast sliders (bacon or sausage) and a Belgian waffle.

Shares of Dutch Bros (BROS) were down 1.7% in premarket trading on Friday. The restaurant stock is off more than 10% over the last six weeks.

data.title: Wall Street volatility spikes as markets tumble amid credit concerns
data.description:
The Cboe Volatility Index (VIX), widely regarded as the market’s “fear gauge,” climbed above 27 on Friday, marking its highest level since April, as investor anxiety spiked amid concerns over the U.S. economy. Loan defaults at two regional banks fueled worries and prompted traders to take profits.

Zions Bancorporation (ZION) dropped 13% on Thursday after reporting a charge-off on a loan issued by a subsidiary, while Western Alliance Bancorp (WAL) fell 11% after disclosing loans to the same borrowers.

The developments echoed memories of the 2023 banking crisis, which started with Silicon Valley Bank’s collapse.

Investors are also watching the private credit sector closely following the failures of subprime auto lender Tricolor Holdings and auto parts supplier First Brands Group. Major banks, including JPMorgan Chase & Co. (JPM) and Jefferies Financial Group (JEF), reported losses linked to these cases.

Banking stocks were set to extend losses on Friday, with Bank of America (BAC) down 1.6% in premarket trading and Morgan Stanley (MS) falling 1.3%.

As a result, stock index futures opened lower, with S&P 500 futures (SPX) down 0.9% following a 1% decline on Thursday, while Nasdaq 100 futures (US100:IND) slipped 1.2%. Nvidia (NVDA) and Tesla (TSLA) were both off more than 2% in premarket trading.

Rising U.S.-China trade tensions are also weighing on sentiment, driving profit-taking after the S&P 500 (SP500) rallied 35% from its April low to an October high. Traders increased Treasury holdings on Friday, pushing 10-year (US10Y) yields down 3 basis points, while gold hit a fresh record high.

Here are some exchange-traded funds focused on volatility: (BATS:VXX), (BATS:VIXY), (VIXX), (BATS:VXZ), (BATS:VIXM), (BATS:UVXY), (BATS:SVXY), and (BATS:UVIX).

data.title: Luminar Technologies is on watch with its founder considering a merger transaction
data.description:
Luminar Technologies (NASDAQ:LAZR) disclosed that founder Austin Russell holds an activist stake of 8.4% in the company. 

Per the SEC filing, Russell, at the suggestion of certain shareholders and the invitation of certain members of the board of directors of Luminar Technologies, sent a non-binding proposal to the board as a preliminary basis for discussion regarding a potential transaction in which Russell AI Labs would acquire 100% of the outstanding shares of the Class A stock of the company.

Russell AI Labs may concurrently acquire a different, larger global automotive technology company, and integrate such business into Luminar (NASDAQ:LAZR) to create a unified technology platform business, referred to as Luminar 2.0. The proposed transaction would see shareholders of the company would receive consideration of cash and/or marketable securities of the combined Luminar 2.0 entity. In connection with the proposed transaction, Russell AI Labs or other entities affiliated with Austin Russell may also consider investment of new capital in the combined company, subject to final terms of the deal.

Austin Russell resigned as president, CEO, and chairperson of Luminar Technologies on May 14 following a business conduct and ethics inquiry by the audit committee of the board of directors

Shares of Luminar Technologies (LAZR) rose 2.2% in premarket trading to $1.93 vs. the 52-week range of $1.58 to $18.60.

data.title: Ucloudlink expands global reach of PetPhone in partnership with Aham Al-Tafasil
data.description:
• Ucloudlink Group (NASDAQ:UCL) announced on Friday the procurement of a 30,000-unit order for PetPhone from Aham Al-Tafasil.
  • This partnership will accelerate PetPhone’s time-to-market, increase delivery speeds, enhance channel efficiency, and strengthen brand visibility throughout Saudi Arabia and the wider Gulf region, marking another significant step in PetPhone’s growing global presence.
